scenario: Cluster on Camunda 8.4. Want to upgrade to 8.6. Wonders version compatibility.
question: Cluster upgrade compatibility?

options.text: 6) are the safest approach, Camunda's release notes may explicitly permit direct upgrades across certain minor version pairs. The key criterion is whether schema migrations, API changes, and data format changes are backward compatible across the skipped versions. The official Camunda documentation and migration guides for each version define the supported upgrade paths. A blanket prohibition on skipping is an overstatement; the actual requirement is to verify the release notes for each specific upgrade path.

Compatibility considerations: when upgrading Camunda 8 Self-Managed, teams must evaluate breaking changes in the BPMN engine, Elasticsearch/OpenSearch schema migrations, Zeebe broker data format compatibility, and API contract changes between source and target versions. Some minor version pairs allow direct upgrade while others require intermediate steps. The official upgrade documentation provides a matrix of supported upgrade paths. Schema migrations may be automatic or require manual execution depending on the version pair. Always review the migration guide and test in non-production environments first.

A full cluster rebuild from scratch is always required for upgrades -- this overstates the disruption involved. Camunda 8 is designed to support rolling upgrades and in-place upgrades for minor version increments. Existing process instances, deployed BPMN models, and accumulated history data are preserved across upgrades. Elasticsearch or OpenSearch schema migrations, when required, are typically handled automatically by the component startup process. A full rebuild would be an extreme measure only applicable in unusual recovery scenarios, not a standard upgrade procedure.

a.text: Incorrect. Camunda 8 upgrade compatibility: Versioning scheme (Camunda 8): Major.Minor.Patch: e.g., 8.4.0. Minor releases: typically backward-compatible. Patch releases: bug fixes; same minor compatibility. Upgrade paths: Minor → next minor: 8.4 → 8.5: typically smooth. 8.5 → 8.6: similar. Skip minor: 8.4 → 8.6: may work; verify per release notes. Recommended: step through (8.4 → 8.5 → 8.6). Major version: 8.x → 9.x: breaking changes likely. Migration path: required. Per Set 13 Q58: cluster upgrade strategies. What to verify per release: Release notes: Breaking changes. Deprecated features. New requirements. BPMN compatibility: Existing models: usually work. Some attributes deprecated: warnings. New features: not breaking. DMN compatibility: Similar to BPMN. Forms compatibility: Schema version: backward typically. API compatibility: gRPC + REST: per version. Deprecated endpoints: marked. SDK compatibility: Camunda 8 client: per cluster version. Older SDK + newer cluster: typically works. Newer SDK + older cluster: may have issues. Connector compatibility: Connectors: per Runtime version. Update Runtime: with cluster. Step-through upgrades: Scenario: 8.4 → 8.7. Approach: 1. Upgrade 8.4 → 8.5. 2. Verify: testing, smoke. 3. Upgrade 8.5 → 8.6. 4. Verify. 5. Upgrade 8.6 → 8.7. Or skip if release notes allow. For testing in staging: Identical to prod: data + config. Run upgrade: validate. Test critical paths: end-to-end. Performance test: regressions. For backup before upgrade (per Set 14 Q55): Critical: rollback if needed. Snapshot data. For BPMN deployment changes: Existing deployed BPMN: should run. New deploys: per new version's features. For in-flight instances: Should continue: across upgrade. Verify: no incidents post-upgrade. For worker compatibility: Workers may need SDK update: depending on version. Test workers: post-upgrade. For Operate / Tasklist data: Schema may evolve: across versions. Automatic migration: typically. Test: data integrity. For Elasticsearch / OpenSearch version: Camunda has compatible ES versions. Verify per Camunda release. For Identity / Keycloak: Compatibility per Camunda version. For Java version: JVM requirements: per Camunda release. Upgrade JVM if needed. For Kubernetes version: Helm chart: per Camunda release. K8s version requirements. For database / storage: Disk space: may increase. Backup retention: review. For monitoring: Metrics may evolve: per version. Grafana dashboards: update. For SaaS: Camunda manages upgrades: in maintenance windows. Customer notified. For Self-Managed: Customer responsibility: plan + execute. Maintenance windows: coordinate. Rolling upgrade (per Set 13 Q58): Node-by-node: minimize downtime. Compatibility during rolling: required. Blue-green: Parallel clusters: cutover. Stronger isolation during upgrade. For canary deployments: Small traffic to new: validate. Camunda 8 cluster: less common. For multi-cluster: Upgrade one cluster first: validate. Then others: rolling org upgrade. For downstream consumers: Coordinate: SDK updates. Communicate: upgrade timeline. For long-running processes: Survive upgrade: typically. Verify: in-flight intact. For workflow processes: No process migration needed: typically. Just engine upgrade. Pre-upgrade checklist: 1. Read release notes. 2. Backup data. 3. Test in staging. 4. Coordinate with stakeholders. 5. Plan rollback. 6. Schedule maintenance. 7. Execute upgrade. 8. Verify production. 9. Monitor closely. For unexpected issues: Rollback to backup: if critical. Investigate: with logs. Camunda support: if SaaS / paid. Post-upgrade verification: Smoke tests: critical paths. Metrics check: latency, throughput. In-flight processes: completing. No new incidents: rate normal. Anti-patterns: Upgrade in prod without staging: high risk. No backup: irreversible. Skip release notes: surprises. Best practices: Stay current: avoid huge jumps. Test thoroughly. Plan for rollback. Communicate. For the scenario (8.4 → 8.6): Read release notes: 8.5 + 8.6. May need step-through: 8.4 → 8.5 → 8.6. Or skip if compatible: verify. Test in staging: critical.
